original_hypothesis.title: Anyone can grief batch poster reimbursements by submitting batches via contract (tx.origin mismatch) to suppress spending reports
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: SequencerInbox only submits batch spending reports when msg.sender == tx.origin (and !isUsingFeeToken). A third party cannot change msg.sender for an honest batch poster, but if a batch poster key is used through a contract wallet / relayer that makes msg.sender != tx.origin, spending report submission is skipped, reducing reimbursement. This can become an operational DoS on batch posting economics if batch posters are forced (by operational setup) to use contract-based posting.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Not an on-chain attacker forcing tx.origin changes, but an operationally realistic pitfall: if batch posting moves to smart accounts/relayers, reimbursements silently stop, potentially affecting liveness. Needs validation against intended posting infrastructure.
deployment_assumptions: Batch posting is performed via smart contract wallets, relayers, or account abstraction where msg.sender != tx.origin
Protocol relies on spending report reimbursements for batch poster economics
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: No on-chain deployment evidence provided for batch poster operational setup; code condition is in SequencerInbox.submitBatchSpendingReport call sites.
preconditions: Batch poster transactions are sent with msg.sender != tx.origin (contract-based execution)
Reimbursement is expected/required for sustainable batch posting

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Economic reimbursements depend on tx.origin checks
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: Continuous if posting infrastructure uses contract senders

operational_questions: Are production batch posters EOAs only, or are smart accounts supported?
How critical are spending reports to batch poster incentives on this deployment?
Are there monitoring alerts when spending reports stop being emitted?
validation_requirements: Confirm operational batch posting path (EOA vs contract)
Quantify reimbursement delta when spending reports are skipped
Test end-to-end that L2 reimbursements depend on these events/messages

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Induce or require batch poster to post through a contract-based path (ops change / migration)
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Measure increased operational cost leading to reduced posting frequency/liveness
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Exploit degraded liveness (secondary effects depend on system)

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Reduced batch posting incentives; potential liveness degradation
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Low; adjust ops to use EOAs or update code to alternative authentication

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Drop in InboxMessageDelivered events corresponding to spending reports
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Batch poster address is a contract; tx.origin differs from msg.sender

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
